source.title: NPNF2-06. Jerome: The Principal Works of St. Jerome

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: Dedicated to Sophronius about the year 392.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: Jerome had, while at Rome, made a translation of the Psalms from the LXX., which he had afterwards corrected by collation with the Hebrew text (see the Preface addressed to Paula and Eustochium, infra).

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: His friend Sophronius, in quoting the Psalms to the Jews, was constantly met with the reply, “It does not so stand in the Hebrew.”

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: He, therefore, urged Jerome to translate them direct from the original.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: Jerome, in presenting the translation to his friend, records the intention which he had expressed of translating the new Latin version into Greek.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: This we know was done by Sophronius, not only for the Psalms, but also for the rest of the Vulgate, and was valued by the Greeks (Apol. ii.

verses.book: Prefaces to the Books of the Vulgate Version of the Old Testament.
verses.text: 24, vol. iii. of this series, p.
